definition:
1. to fall under  or down, to sink in , (Plutarch) 
2. to fall down before  any one, with accusative, (Plato Philosophus): —;hence, to be subject  to him, fall under  his power, (Isocrates Orator); of a flatterer, to cringe to , τινί (Demosthenes Orator); with accusative ὑποπεσὼν τὸν δεσπότην (Aristophanes Comicus) 
3. to fall behind  another, (Demosthenes Orator) 
4. to get in under  or among , (Thucydides) 
5. of accidents, to happen, fall out , (Isocrates Orator) 
6. of persons, to fall under  a punishment, with dative, (Plutarch) 
7. of places, to lie under  or below , with dative, (Polybius Historicus) (ML)
